title: Server File Download Flow
description: Handling an individual skill/command file download request across two runtime environments: Bun dev server and Cloudflare Workers (edge). Both expose GET /api/download/:type/:provider/:id but differ in how files are resolved.
interaction_flow_bun_server: BunServer → handleFileDownload(type, provider, id): route match extracts params
handleFileDownload → getFilePath(type, provider, id): resolve disk path
getFilePath: returns dist/{provider}/{configDir}/skills/{id}/SKILL.md (or null if unknown provider)
handleFileDownload → Client: Response(content, 200) headers: Content-Type=application/octet-stream, Content-Disposition=attachment; filename='SKILL.md'
interaction_flow_cf_worker: onRequestGet: validate type !== 'skill' && type !== 'command' → Response.json({error:'Invalid type'}, 400)
onRequestGet → CF_ASSETS.fetch(url): fetch static asset from Cloudflare ASSETS binding

onRequestGet → Client: Response(content, 200) headers: Content-Type=application/octet-stream, Content-Disposition=attachment; filename='SKILL.md', Cache-Control=public s-maxage=86400 stale-while-revalidate=3600
key_relationships: handleFileDownload depends_on validation.js (isAllowedType, isAllowedFileProvider, isValidId, sanitizeFilename)

validation_rules.provider: must be in FILE_DOWNLOAD_PROVIDERS: [cursor, claude-code, gemini, codex, agents, kiro, opencode, pi]
validation_rules.sanitizeFilename: strips all chars except [a-zA-Z0-9._-] from filename for Content-Disposition header

response_shape.error_400: Invalid type | Invalid provider | Invalid file ID
response_shape.error_500: Error downloading file (Bun server only, on readFile exception)

important_notes: Two parallel implementations exist: (1) Bun dev server reads from local disk via fs/promises, (2) Cloudflare Worker fetches from static ASSETS binding — no disk access. The CF worker uses inline validation (VALID_ID regex + FILE_DOWNLOAD_PROVIDERS array directly) while the Bun server delegates all validation to server/lib/validation.js. Both converge on the same path template: dist/{provider}/{configDir}/skills/{id}/SKILL.md. Type 'command' is accepted as alias for 'skill'.
